campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to accept a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue and voted on extending a temporary local ordinance.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth scholarships
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
City applying for grant funding to study the bridge project
Temporary local ordinance extended

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council voted on which community organizations and events would receive grant money from the city budget. They approved funding for specific groups that applied for grants, decided how much money the competitive event grant program would have available, and made changes to the rules for how community events can get funding and close streets.
keyTopics: Approved grant recipients and how much money each group gets
Set total budget for the Competitive Event Grant Program
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including cable TV, online streaming, and phone call-in options. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages to make it accessible to the city's diverse population.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting: Cable Channel 26, YouTube, city website, or Zoom
Live translations available in 50+ languages through Wordly
Phone dial-in option available for those without internet access
Meeting accessible but public comment period was not included in this agenda notice

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been called off. The next chance to discuss zoning issues with the city will be on April 8, 2026 instead.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects. The group approved previous meeting notes and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support housing and services for residents in need.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding available for affordable housing projects
Community development grant applications being reviewed
Support services funding for vulnerable residents
Commission approval of previous meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed ways to encourage shop owners to get proper licenses, promote local shopping through summer events, and offer financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping unlicensed businesses get legitimate without penalties
Outreach to shopping centers to increase participation in city programs
Summer of Celebrations campaign to drive customers to local shops
Public-private partnerships to fund city events
Financial incentives to attract retail and startup businesses to Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026 where they dealt with several postponed items and held two public hearings. The council approved a plan to remove weeds around the city as part of the county's 2026 weed abatement program, and residents had a chance to comment on how federal housing grant money should be spent in the city for the coming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Weed removal program across Santa Clara for 2026
Federal housing grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Multiple agenda items postponed or reconsidered
Public comment opportunity on housing and development funds

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine business items. The council recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement allowing the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract to purchase email security software (Mimecast services) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
